---
title: "GEX_liftover"
output: html_document
date: "2025-01-24"
Author : Manasa Kalya
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

# GitHub-ready relative paths. Run this script from the repository root.
project_dir <- getwd()
input_dir <- file.path(project_dir, "input")
output_dir <- file.path(project_dir, "output")
plots_dir <- file.path(output_dir, "plots")
functions_dir <- file.path(project_dir, "functions")

dir.create(input_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create(plots_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r Libraries}
# Install necessary package (if not installed)

library("GenomicRanges")
library(rtracklayer)
library(GenomicRanges)
library(rtracklayer)
library(readr)
library(tximport)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)

#install.packages("R.utils")
library(R.utils)

```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r Load hg38 gtf file, echo=FALSE}

# Load the GTF file for hg38
gtf_hg38 <- import(file.path(input_dir, "gencode.v42.primary_assembly.annotation.gtf.gz"))

# Extract relevant information: gene names and coordinates (chromosome, start, end)
# Extract gene entries (type == "gene")
gene_gr <- gtf_hg38[gtf_hg38$type == "gene"]

# Extract chromosome, start, and end coordinates
seqnames <- seqnames(gene_gr)
start_coords <- start(gene_gr)
end_coords <- end(gene_gr)

# Extract gene metadata (gene_id and gene_name)
gene_metadata <- mcols(gene_gr)[, c("gene_id", "gene_name")]

# Combine the extracted information into a data frame
gene_data <- data.frame(
  seqnames = as.character(seqnames),
  start = start_coords,
  end = end_coords,
  gene_id = gene_metadata$gene_id,
  gene_name = gene_metadata$gene_name
)

# View the first few rows of the gene data
head(gene_data)

```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

```{r Download and access hg38Tohg19 liftover-chain, echo=FALSE}

# Doownload liftover chain hg38tohg19
# wget http://hgdownload.cse.ucsc.edu/goldenpath/hg38/liftOver/hg38ToHg19.over.chain.gz

# Unzip the chain file if it is in .gz format
# gunzip("hg38ToHg19.over.chain.gz", overwrite = TRUE)

# Load the liftOver chain file for hg38 to hg19

chain_file <- import.chain(file.path(input_dir, "hg38ToHg19.over.chain"))

# Perform the liftOver conversion
lifted_gr <- liftOver(gene_gr, chain_file)  #Check if all gene_gr is availkable in hg19 chain fail 

```


```{r further processing hg38Tohg19 liftover-chain, echo=FALSE}

# If lifted_gr is a GRangesList, we need to unlist it correctly                           ####

if (inherits(lifted_gr, "GRangesList")) {                                                 ####
  # Unlist the GRangesList into a single GRanges object                                   ####
  lifted_gr <- unlist(lifted_gr, use.names = FALSE)                                       ####
}

# Now you can check the number of NA values again in lifted_gr
num_failed <- sum(is.na(lifted_gr))
cat("Number of failed liftover conversions:", num_failed, "\n")

# If there are failed entries, print them
if (num_failed > 0) {
  failed_liftover <- lifted_gr[is.na(lifted_gr)]
  cat("Failed liftover conversions:\n")
  print(failed_liftover)
} else {
  cat("No failed liftover conversions.\n")
}

# Says 0 missing in liftover ... 

# Check the lifted GRanges object
head(lifted_gr)

```

```{r Loading GEX table for RODAM project (hg38), echo=FALSE}


expr_data <- read.csv(file.path(input_dir, "allsamples_normalized_filtered(10countsin1)_DESeq2.csv"))

# gene identifier in expr_data and dmrs_gr should be same
rownames(expr_data) <- expr_data$X
# expr_data <- expr_data[, !colnames(expr_data) %in% "X"]

```



```{r Liftover GEX table for RODAM project (hg38), echo=FALSE}

# Extract Ensembl gene IDs from lifted GRanges object
lifted_gene_ids <- as.data.frame(lifted_gr)$gene_id

# Extract hg19 gene information (chromosome, start, end) from lifted_gr
lifted_seqnames <- seqnames(lifted_gr)
lifted_start_coords <- start(lifted_gr)
lifted_end_coords <- end(lifted_gr)
lifted_gene_ids <- as.data.frame(lifted_gr)$gene_id     # has version number 
lifted_gene_ids <- sub("\\.\\d+$", "", lifted_gene_ids)  # to remove version number to match expr_data

lifted_gene_name <- as.data.frame(lifted_gr)$gene_name


# Initialize new columns for the final dataframe
#expr_data$hg38_ensembl_id <- NA
#expr_data$hg19_ensembl_id <- NA


# Debug: Check how many Ensembl IDs are in the DEG table
cat("Number of rows in Expression table:", nrow(expr_data), "\n")

# If lifted_gr is a GRangesList, we need to unlist it correctly
if (inherits(lifted_gr, "GRangesList")) {
  # Unlist the GRangesList into a single GRanges object
  lifted_gr <- unlist(lifted_gr, use.names = FALSE)
}

# Now you can check the number of NA values again in lifted_gr
num_failed <- sum(is.na(lifted_gr))
cat("Number of failed liftover conversions:", num_failed, "\n")

# If there are failed entries, print them
if (num_failed > 0) {
  failed_liftover <- lifted_gr[is.na(lifted_gr)]
  cat("Failed liftover conversions:\n")
  print(failed_liftover)
} else {
  cat("No failed liftover conversions.\n")
}



#expr_data$hg38_ensembl_id[rownames(expr_data) %in% gene_data$gene_id] <- gene_data$gene_id    #gene_data is hg38
# expr_data$hg19_ensembl_id[rownames(expr_data) %in% mcols(lifted_gr)$gene_id] <- mcols(lifted_gr)$gene_id   #lifted_gr is hg38 liftedover to hg19

# Create a 'gene_id' column in expr_data using rownames (for merging with hg38)
expr_data$gene_id <- rownames(expr_data)
# Now merge expr_data with gene_data using 'gene_id' column (merge hg38 information)
hg38_expr_data <- merge(expr_data, gene_data, by = "gene_id", all.x = TRUE)
hg38_expr_data$hg38_ensembl_id <- hg38_expr_data$gene_id


lifted_gr_df <- as.data.frame(lifted_gr)

# Check if gene_id exists in the metadata columns of lifted_gr
# If "gene_id" is part of the metadata columns, use it directly.
# Otherwise, adjust this to the correct column name.
lifted_gr_df <- lifted_gr_df[, c("gene_id", "seqnames", "start", "end")]   #39694 duplicates

# Aggregate by gene_id, taking the first row for each gene_id
lifted_gr_df_aggregated <- lifted_gr_df %>%
  group_by(gene_id) %>%
  summarise(
    seqnames = first(seqnames),
    start = first(start),
    end = first(end)
  )
# 
# # Perform the merge (left join on gene_id) to include hg19 information
# hg1938_expr_data <- merge(hg38_expr_data, lifted_gr_df_aggregated, by = "gene_id", all.x = TRUE)
# hg1938_expr_data$hg19_ensembl_id   <- hg1938_expr_data$gene_id
# 
# # View the merged result
# head(hg1938_expr_data)

# Merge hg38_expr_data with lifted_gr_df based on gene_id
hg19_expr_data <- merge(hg38_expr_data, lifted_gr_df[, c("gene_id", "seqnames", "start", "end")], by = c("gene_id"), all.x = TRUE)

# Optional: Add hg19 Ensembl ID column, same as gene_id (if necessary)
hg19_expr_data$hg19_ensembl_id <- hg19_expr_data$gene_id

# write.csv(hg19_expr_data, "liftOver_hg38tohg19_dataframe.csv")

# preparing final dataframe for eqtm analysis
# Correctly define the columns to remove
rm <- c("seqnames.x", "seqnames.y", "start.x", "start.y", "end.x", "end.y", "hg38_ensembl_id", "gene_id", 'gene_name')

# Remove the specified columns
df9 <- hg19_expr_data[, !colnames(hg19_expr_data) %in% rm]     

library(dplyr)

summarized_df9 <- df9 %>%
  group_by(hg19_ensembl_id) %>%  # Group by 'hg19_ensembl_id'
  summarise(across(everything(),  # Apply function to all columns
                   mean,           # Only calculate the mean
                   na.rm = TRUE),  # Ignore NA values
            .groups = 'drop')  # Drop the grouping after summarizing

# View the summarized data
head(summarized_df9)

write.csv(summarized_df9, file.path(output_dir, "CKD_hg19_filteredNormalizedGEX_touse.csv"))

#write.csv(hg1938_expr_data, "./processedGEX_liftover_hg38Tohg19.csv")

```






```{r junk_1, echo=FALSE}

# Extract Ensembl gene IDs from lifted GRanges object
lifted_gene_ids <- as.data.frame(lifted_gr)$gene_id

# Extract hg19 gene information (chromosome, start, end) from lifted_gr
lifted_seqnames <- seqnames(lifted_gr)
lifted_start_coords <- start(lifted_gr)
lifted_end_coords <- end(lifted_gr)
lifted_gene_ids <- as.data.frame(lifted_gr)$gene_id
lifted_gene_name <- as.data.frame(lifted_gr)$gene_name

# Initialize new columns for the final dataframe
expr_data$hg38_ensembl_id <- NA
expr_data$hg19_ensembl_id <- NA

# Debug: Check how many Ensembl IDs are in the DEG table
cat("Number of rows in DEG table:", nrow(expr_data), "\n")

# If lifted_gr is a GRangesList, we need to unlist it correctly
if (inherits(lifted_gr, "GRangesList")) {
  # Unlist the GRangesList into a single GRanges object
  lifted_gr <- unlist(lifted_gr, use.names = FALSE)
}

# Now you can check the number of NA values again in lifted_gr
num_failed <- sum(is.na(lifted_gr))
cat("Number of failed liftover conversions:", num_failed, "\n")

# If there are failed entries, print them
if (num_failed > 0) {
  failed_liftover <- lifted_gr[is.na(lifted_gr)]
  cat("Failed liftover conversions:\n")
  print(failed_liftover)
} else {
  cat("No failed liftover conversions.\n")
}

# Create a 'gene_id' column in expr_data using rownames (for merging with hg38)
expr_data$gene_id <- rownames(expr_data)

# Now merge expr_data with gene_data using 'gene_id' column (merge hg38 information)
hg38_expr_data <- merge(expr_data, gene_data, by = "gene_id", all.x = TRUE)

# Extract the gene_id from lifted_gr and convert to a data.frame
lifted_gr_df <- as.data.frame(lifted_gr)

# Check if gene_id exists in the metadata columns of lifted_gr
# If "gene_id" is part of the metadata columns, use it directly.
# Otherwise, adjust this to the correct column name.
lifted_gr_df <- lifted_gr_df[, c("gene_id", "seqnames", "start", "end")]

# Perform the merge (left join on gene_id) to include hg19 information
hg1938_expr_data <- merge(hg38_expr_data, lifted_gr_df, by = "gene_id", all.x = TRUE)

# View the merged result
head(hg1938_expr_data)



# Sanity check 
common_gene_ids <- intersect(unique_gene_ids_expr, unique_gene_ids_gene_data)
cat("Number of common gene_ids between expr_data and gene_data:", length(common_gene_ids), "\n")
# will print 20328
common_gene_ids_lifted <- intersect(unique(expr_data$gene_id), unique(lifted_gr_df$gene_id))
cat("Number of common gene_ids between expr_data and lifted_gr:", length(common_gene_ids_lifted), "\n")
# will print 20308 

# Few genes are not lifted over, lets check why? 
# Find gene_ids in expr_data that are not in lifted_gr
missing_gene_ids <- setdiff(rownames(expr_data), lifted_gr_df$gene_id)
cat("Number of missing gene_ids in liftover:", length(missing_gene_ids), "\n")
# Extract rows from expr_data where gene_ids are missing in lifted_gr
# Check missing gene_ids in gene_data
missing_in_gene_data <- missing_gene_ids[!(missing_gene_ids %in% gene_data$gene_id)]

# Print any missing gene_ids from gene_data
cat("Missing gene_ids in gene_data:", length(missing_in_gene_data), "\n")

```
```{r junk_2, echo=FALSE}

# Merge hg38_expr_data with lifted_gr_df based on gene_id
hg19_expr_data <- merge(hg38_expr_data, lifted_gr_df[, c("gene_id", "seqnames", "start", "end")], by = "gene_id", all.x = TRUE)

# Optional: Add hg19 Ensembl ID column, same as gene_id (if necessary)
hg19_expr_data$hg19_ensembl_id <- hg19_expr_data$gene_id

# write.csv(hg19_expr_data, "liftOver_hg38tohg19_dataframe.csv")

# preparing final dataframe for eqtm analysis
# Correctly define the columns to remove
rm <- c("seqnames.x", "seqnames.y", "start.x", "start.y", "end.x", "end.y", "hg38_ensembl_id", "gene_id", 'gene_name')

# Remove the specified columns
df9 <- hg19_expr_data[, !colnames(hg19_expr_data) %in% rm]     

library(dplyr)

summarized_df9 <- df9 %>%
  select(-hg19_ensembl_id) %>%  # Exclude 'hg19_ensembl_id' column
  summarise(across(everything(),  # Apply function to all remaining columns
                   mean,           # Only calculate the mean
                   na.rm = TRUE))  # Ignore NA values

# View the summarized data
head(summarized_df9)


rownames(df9) <- hg19_expr_data$hg19_ensembl_id    #duplicate rownames are not allowed



```
